background.claim_boundary: v5.11 constructs the strongest source-qualified operator architecture and a finite gauge-bookkeeping diagnostic. Missing action sources, gauges, domains, boundary conditions, and eta data prevent a determinant-ready full complex.

rows.boundary_data: outward collar convention; detailed geometric data open
rows.domain: compact Berger-Hopf boundary plus normalized collar and its normal bundle
rows.equation: delta S/delta g=0 and normal shape equation E_perp=0
rows.source: v5.4 symbolic geometric slot plus v5.6 boundary/collar action

background.tadpole_rule: Retain unresolved first variations explicitly; do not interpret off-shell quadratic eigenvalues as physical masses or stability data.

boundary_adjoint.claim_boundary: v5.11 constructs the strongest source-qualified operator architecture and a finite gauge-bookkeeping diagnostic. Missing action sources, gauges, domains, boundary conditions, and eta data prevent a determinant-ready full complex.
boundary_adjoint.extension_ambiguity: multiple standard candidate families; none selected using phenomenology

rows.boundary_form: Green form from L_geom plus F boundary terms
Green form of H_surface; fourth-order pieces require two edge/collar conditions
rows.candidate: physical normal-mode domain with collar matching
rows.ghost_compatible: xi_perp must not be paired with a ghost unless proven pure gauge

rows.boundary_form: <a,D_n b>-<D_n a,b> plus ghost scalar form
rows.dense_domain: standard candidate only, BHSM selection open

rows.boundary_form: delta T partial_n delta T-delta T' partial_n delta T plus Phi analogue
rows.selected: only on declared homogeneous reduced domain

boundary_stress_tensor.K2_and_shape_pieces: functional metric/embedding variations of c_K2 K^2 and c_S Tr(S^2), including derivative stresses generated by delta K and delta S_ab
boundary_stress_tensor.K_piece: c_K times the orientation-dependent Brown-York combination (K^ab-K gamma^ab), plus embedding terms when the hypersurface moves
boundary_stress_tensor.collar_piece: functional variation of B_threshold J with respect to gamma_ab and S_ab
boundary_stress_tensor.pure_tension_piece: -T_boundary gamma^ab under the declared stress convention
boundary_stress_tensor.quantum_piece: not a local stress tensor from v5.10; only a controlled uniform-mode diagnostic exists
boundary_stress_tensor.scalar_topographic_piece: on-shell boundary stress/pressure from U_boundary and collar threshold terms

boundary_tension_candidate.dimension: energy per boundary area (or action per boundary volume) only after the action measure and units are fixed
boundary_tension_candidate.physical_sign: unresolved; the normalized -1/8 is not by itself a signed physical tension
boundary_tension_candidate.reason: v5.7 evaluates the total normalized reduced action, not the local U_boundary density or its area normalization

boundary_tension_surface_mode.claim_boundary: v5.11 constructs the strongest source-qualified operator architecture and a finite gauge-bookkeeping diagnostic. Missing action sources, gauges, domains, boundary conditions, and eta data prevent a determinant-ready full complex.

coefficient_provenance.c_J: no separate log J term in v5.7 to avoid double counting; the action-derived collar Jacobian inside S_collar remains active
coefficient_provenance.c_K: symbolic v5.6 boundary-action coefficient; v5.7 zero meant no scalar variation on fixed normalized geometry, not a geometric zero
coefficient_provenance.c_K2: symbolic v5.6 boundary-action coefficient; not set to zero in normal shape variation
coefficient_provenance.c_S: symbolic v5.6 boundary-action coefficient; not set to zero in normal shape variation

critical_scale_classification.fixed_in_terms_of_one_primitive_tension_: possible only conditionally after an absolute density and remaining relative coefficients/mode functions are sourced
critical_scale_classification.reason: -1/8 and the v5.7 coefficient zeros are normalized scalar-reduction data; they do not provide dimensionful surface density or geometric shape coefficients

lowest_mode.definition: lambda_surface(L,a,sigma,rho_star)=inf spec'(H_surface) after proven tangential-gauge and Killing kernels are removed
lowest_mode.mode_functions: q_tau,q_K,q_K2,q_S require the Berger/collar embedding and normal-mode domain

normal_displacement.domain: normal bundle over the Berger-Hopf boundary; self-adjoint domain open
normal_displacement.gauge_separation: boundary-preserving tangential diffeomorphisms are gauge; physical normal motion requires a declared embedding/boundary rule

normal_shape_equation.collar_jacobian: E_J from delta J in the collar measure, not discarded with c_J=0
normal_shape_equation.external_pressure_jump: Delta p retained symbolically; no cosmological input
normal_shape_equation.scalar_topographic_pressure: p_ST from normal variation of on-shell U_boundary and threshold support

preserved_primordial_interpretation.status: candidate; positive lowest physical surface eigenvalue required
candidate release threshold; not yet solved
conditional on a negative crossing and correct negative-mode treatment
interpretive downstream state, not derived or observationally validated here

quadratic_operator.boundary_form: requires the xi_perp domain and edge/collar matching; not closed
quadratic_operator.principal_order: second order for pure tension/K architecture; K^2 and Tr(S^2) variations can produce fourth-order boundary terms

quantum_term.normal_projection: H_quantum=J_L^dagger L^-2 J_L plus connection/tadpole terms only if a uniform xi_perp-to-delta L map is derived
quantum_term.reason: v5.10 is a renormalization-scale-dependent partial diagnostic, not a local quantum stress tensor
quantum_term.valid_v5_10_scope: Gamma_perp=1/2 log[6/(mu^2 L^2)] for one homogeneous scalar mode

variation_convention.collar_jacobian: delta log J=Tr[(I+rho S)^(-1) rho delta S] for J=det(I+rho S), with sign-equivalent opposite-normal convention
variation_convention.shape_norm: D_Q xi_perp=delta_xi Tr(S^2), computed from delta S_ab and delta gamma_ab in the selected collar orientation

charged.classification: current-current/projected fermion bilinear; not an independent propagating field

charged_neutral.claim_boundary: v5.11 constructs the strongest source-qualified operator architecture and a finite gauge-bookkeeping diagnostic. Missing action sources, gauges, domains, boundary conditions, and eta data prevent a determinant-ready full complex.

neutral.auxiliary_elimination: conditional on an invertible normalized K_neu and a closed domain
neutral.classification: symbolic response/auxiliary candidate; action does not decide independent versus effective

claim_boundary: v5.11 constructs the strongest source-qualified operator architecture and a finite gauge-bookkeeping diagnostic. Missing action sources, gauges, domains, boundary conditions, and eta data prevent a determinant-ready full complex.
claim_safe_conclusion: BHSM v5.11 retains the vacuum boundary/collar value as a candidate tension source and derives a formal normal release equation, but the absolute density, shape coefficients, embedding, and spectrum remain open. The threshold is still scale covariant. No full one-loop action, Casimir energy, absolute unit, mass, coupling, CKM result, rare-B prediction, physical validation, or BHSM completion follows.
conditionally_established: the normalized -1/8 vacuum value is retained as a candidate boundary/collar contribution but is not identified with an absolute surface density
a finite L_c can arise from ratios of independently sourced dimensionful tension/shape coefficients and mode functions
formal principal symbols become elliptic under conventional gauges before boundary completion
D_BHSM and D^dagger D have Dirac/Laplace principal symbols if the missing source and domain close
neutral auxiliary elimination requires an invertible normalized K_neu
derived: canonical full field/symmetry and all-36-block source-status maps
conditional geometric/internal FP formulas from explicitly conventional gauges
normal shape equation and surface-Hessian decomposition with tension, K, K^2, Tr(S^2), collar, scalar pressure, and controlled quantum slots
exact homogeneous T/Phi Hessian eigenvalues 4/L^2 and 6/L^2
charged current has no independent determinant in the stored composite classification
finite gauge/ghost consistency model with gauge-parameter-independent physical Hessian and normalized quotient

invalidated_or_ruled_out: v5.7 c_K=c_K2=c_S=0 in a fixed scalar reduction does not prove the geometric coefficients vanish
the normalized vacuum value -1/8 cannot by itself be promoted to a physical boundary tension
the current surface release condition does not fix an absolute L_c
the present repository does not support a full determinant-ready operator complex
standard gauge or boundary candidates cannot be relabeled BHSM-derived
the v5.10 one-mode determinant is not the official or full one-loop action
the earlier curvature-threshold mass-gap theorem remains invalidated
unresolved negative and zero modes cannot be silently removed
